title: TC-CUSTOM-EQ-001: Custom EQ slider persistence after Save EQ and screen re-entry
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name (e.g., Surround 85 or Free 60+).
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom EQ' preset to activate it.
Adjust each EQ slider to distinct non-default values: Set Band 1 (Low) to 80, Band 2 (Mid-Low) to 60, Band 3 (Mid) to 40, Band 4 (Mid-High) to 70, Band 5 (High) to 90.
Tap the 'Save EQ' button.
Tap the back arrow (<-) to return to Audio Settings screen.
Tap Equalizer again to reopen the Equalizer screen.
Observe each of the 5 EQ slider positions.
expected: All 5 EQ slider positions are retained exactly as saved: Band 1 = 80, Band 2 = 60, Band 3 = 40, Band 4 = 70, Band 5 = 90. Sliders are not reset to default (flat/neutral) positions.

title: TC-CUSTOM-EQ-002: Custom EQ slider persistence after Save EQ, app kill, and relaunch
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name (e.g., Surround 85).
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom EQ' preset to activate it.
Adjust each EQ slider to distinct non-default values: Set Band 1 (Low) to 20, Band 2 (Mid-Low) to 85, Band 3 (Mid) to 55, Band 4 (Mid-High) to 30, Band 5 (High) to 75.
Tap the 'Save EQ' button.
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe away from recent apps or use OS settings to force stop).
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Wait for the app to reconnect to the device (verify device is shown as connected).
Tap on the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe each of the 5 EQ slider positions.
expected: All 5 EQ slider positions are retained after app kill and relaunch: Band 1 = 20, Band 2 = 85, Band 3 = 55, Band 4 = 30, Band 5 = 75. The Custom EQ preset is still selected and sliders match the saved values.

title: TC-CUSTOM-EQ-003: Custom EQ slider persistence after Save EQ, Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect cycle
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name (e.g., Free 60+).
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom EQ' preset to activate it.
Adjust each EQ slider to distinct non-default values: Set Band 1 (Low) to 100, Band 2 (Mid-Low) to 50, Band 3 (Mid) to 0, Band 4 (Mid-High) to 75, Band 5 (High) to 25.
Tap the 'Save EQ' button.
Disconnect the Bluetooth device (turn off Bluetooth on the phone or put the device out of range).
Wait 10 seconds.
Reconnect the Bluetooth device (turn Bluetooth back on or bring device back in range).
Wait for the app to show the device as connected.
Tap on the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe each of the 5 EQ slider positions.
expected: All 5 EQ slider positions are retained after Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect: Band 1 = 100, Band 2 = 50, Band 3 = 0, Band 4 = 75, Band 5 = 25. The Custom EQ preset is still selected and sliders match the saved values.

title: TC-CUSTOM-EQ-004: Custom EQ slider persistence after Save EQ, device power off/on cycle
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name (e.g., Surround 85).
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom EQ' preset to activate it.
Adjust each EQ slider to distinct non-default values: Set Band 1 (Low) to 70, Band 2 (Mid-Low) to 30, Band 3 (Mid) to 90, Band 4 (Mid-High) to 10, Band 5 (High) to 60.
Tap the 'Save EQ' button.
Power off the Bluetooth device using its power button.
Wait 15 seconds.
Power on the Bluetooth device.
Wait for the device to reconnect to the phone (verify connection in app).
Tap on the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe each of the 5 EQ slider positions.
expected: All 5 EQ slider positions are retained after device power off/on cycle: Band 1 = 70, Band 2 = 30, Band 3 = 90, Band 4 = 10, Band 5 = 60. The Custom EQ preset is still selected and sliders match the saved values.

title: TC-CUSTOM-EQ-005: Custom EQ slider persistence — Save EQ vs. screen exit without Save (state trigger boundary test)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name (e.g., Free 60+).
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom EQ' preset to activate it.
Adjust each EQ slider to distinct non-default values: Set Band 1 (Low) to 45, Band 2 (Mid-Low) to 65, Band 3 (Mid) to 85, Band 4 (Mid-High) to 25, Band 5 (High) to 55.
Do NOT tap 'Save EQ'.
Tap the back arrow (<-) to return to Audio Settings screen.
Tap Equalizer again to reopen the Equalizer screen.
Observe each of the 5 EQ slider positions.
Now adjust sliders to new values: Band 1 (Low) to 10, Band 2 (Mid-Low) to 90, Band 3 (Mid) to 35, Band 4 (Mid-High) to 80, Band 5 (High) to 15.
Tap the 'Save EQ' button.
Tap the back arrow (<-) to return to Audio Settings screen.
Tap Equalizer again to reopen the Equalizer screen.
Observe each of the 5 EQ slider positions.
expected: First observation (Step 9): Sliders may or may not retain the unsaved values depending on whether the app auto-saves on exit — this documents the current behavior. Second observation (Step 14): After tapping 'Save EQ', all 5 slider positions are retained exactly as saved: Band 1 = 10, Band 2 = 90, Band 3 = 35, Band 4 = 80, Band 5 = 15. This confirms that the 'Save EQ' button is the definitive persistence trigger.

title: TC-CUSTOM-EQ-006: Device asymmetry — Custom EQ slider persistence on Surround 85 vs. Free 60+ after app kill
steps: **Part A — Surround 85:**
Connect a Surround 85 device to the phone.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on Surround 85 → Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap 'Custom EQ' preset.
Set sliders to: Band 1=85, Band 2=40, Band 3=60, Band 4=20, Band 5=95.
Tap 'Save EQ'.
Force-kill the app.
Reopen app, reconnect, navigate to Equalizer.
Record all slider positions.
**Part B — Free 60+:**
Connect a Free 60+ device to the phone.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on Free 60+ → Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap 'Custom EQ' preset.
Set sliders to: Band 1=85, Band 2=40, Band 3=60, Band 4=20, Band 5=95.
Tap 'Save EQ'.
Force-kill the app.
Reopen app, reconnect, navigate to Equalizer.
Record all slider positions.
**Part C — Compare:** Compare the slider positions from Part A (Step 10) and Part B (Step 19).
expected: Both Surround 85 and Free 60+ should retain all 5 Custom EQ slider positions after app kill and relaunch. If one device loses slider positions while the other retains them, this confirms a device asymmetry firmware bug. Document any differences in behavior between the two devices.

title: TC-CUSTOM-EQ-007: Custom EQ slider persistence after switching between EQ presets and back to Custom
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name (e.g., Surround 85).
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom EQ' preset to activate it.
Adjust each EQ slider to distinct non-default values: Set Band 1 (Low) to 75, Band 2 (Mid-Low) to 25, Band 3 (Mid) to 100, Band 4 (Mid-High) to 45, Band 5 (High) to 80.
Tap the 'Save EQ' button.
Tap the 'Bass Boost' preset.
Observe that sliders change to the Bass Boost curve.
Tap the 'Custom EQ' preset again.
Observe each of the 5 EQ slider positions.
expected: When switching back to 'Custom EQ' preset, all 5 slider positions are restored to the previously saved values: Band 1 = 75, Band 2 = 25, Band 3 = 100, Band 4 = 45, Band 5 = 80. Switching to another preset and back does not reset the Custom EQ slider values.

title: TC-CUSTOM-EQ-008: Custom EQ slider persistence after phone rotation during EQ editing
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name (e.g., Free 60+).
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom EQ' preset to activate it.
Adjust each EQ slider to distinct non-default values: Set Band 1 (Low) to 30, Band 2 (Mid-Low) to 70, Band 3 (Mid) to 20, Band 4 (Mid-High) to 90, Band 5 (High) to 40.
Tap the 'Save EQ' button.
Rotate the phone from portrait to landscape (or vice versa).
Observe the Equalizer screen after rotation.
Tap the back arrow (<-) to return to Audio Settings.
Tap Equalizer again to reopen the Equalizer screen.
Observe each of the 5 EQ slider positions.
expected: After phone rotation, the Equalizer screen displays correctly with all 5 slider positions retained: Band 1 = 30, Band 2 = 70, Band 3 = 20, Band 4 = 90, Band 5 = 40. Navigating away and back also shows the saved values unchanged.

title: TC-CUSTOM-EQ-009: Custom EQ slider persistence — rapid Save EQ and immediate app backgrounding
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name (e.g., Surround 85).
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom EQ' preset to activate it.
Adjust each EQ slider to distinct non-default values: Set Band 1 (Low) to 55, Band 2 (Mid-Low) to 35, Band 3 (Mid) to 80, Band 4 (Mid-High) to 15, Band 5 (High) to 65.
Tap the 'Save EQ' button.
Immediately press the Home button to background the app (within 1 second of tapping Save).
Wait 5 seconds.
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app from recent apps.
Tap on the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe each of the 5 EQ slider positions.
expected: All 5 EQ slider positions are retained despite the rapid backgrounding after Save: Band 1 = 55, Band 2 = 35, Band 3 = 80, Band 4 = 15, Band 5 = 65. The Save operation completes successfully before the app is backgrounded, or the save is atomic and completes on relaunch.

title: TC-CUSTOM-EQ-010: Custom EQ slider boundary values persistence after full lifecycle
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap on the connected device name (e.g., Free 60+).
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom EQ' preset to activate it.
Set all 5 EQ sliders to their minimum value (0).
Tap the 'Save EQ' button.
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Reopen app, reconnect, navigate to Equalizer.
Verify all sliders are at 0.
Now set all 5 EQ sliders to their maximum value (100).
Tap the 'Save EQ' button.
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Reopen app, reconnect, navigate to Equalizer.
Verify all sliders are at 100.
Now set sliders to mixed boundary values: Band 1=0, Band 2=100, Band 3=0, Band 4=100, Band 5=50.
Tap the 'Save EQ' button.
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Reopen app, reconnect, navigate to Equalizer.
Verify each slider position matches: Band 1=0, Band 2=100, Band 3=0, Band 4=100, Band 5=50.
expected: All boundary values (minimum 0, maximum 100, and mixed boundaries) persist correctly across app kill and relaunch cycles. No slider snaps to a default value or gets clamped incorrectly.
